symbols.name: HandlerRequest
symbols.description: What the handler sees — decrypted request data plus context. No envelope concerns (clientPublicKeyHex, replyTo are handled by respondTo).

symbols.name: Handler
symbols.description: A handler — the portable unit of backend logic. Takes a request, returns a response. Knows nothing about transport or encryption.

symbols.name: Identity
symbols.description: Identity — signing and encryption keypairs for a service.

symbols.name: Connection
symbols.description: A connection to a remote node.

symbols.name: respondTo
symbols.signature: function respondTo<TReq, TRes>(handler: Handler<TReq, TRes>, config: unknown): (msg: unknown) => Promise<unknown>
symbols.description: Wrap a handler as a compose-compatible processor.

symbols.name: connect
symbols.signature: function connect(client: ProtocolInterfaceNode, config: unknown): Connection
symbols.description: Bridge a processor to a remote node via polling.

symbols.name: writeRequest
symbols.description: Write an encrypted request to a handler's inbox. This is what a client calls to send a request.

symbols.name: readResponse
symbols.signature: async function readResponse<T>(params: unknown): Promise<unknown | null>
symbols.description: Read and decrypt a response from a handler's outbox. This is what a client calls after sending a request.
